top_categories.workout_context: 370 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
370 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 370 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Bordeaux-Paris Ultra Cycling Challenge's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Bordeaux-Paris Ultra Cycling Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Tempo is Bordeaux-Paris Ultra Cycling Challenge's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 370 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
Bordeaux-Paris Ultra Cycling Challenge's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 370 miles.
Bordeaux-Paris Ultra Cycling Challenge's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Bordeaux-Paris Ultra Cycling Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 370 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 370 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 370 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, HVLI Extended, and Endurance to prepare you for 370 miles of mixed terrain in Bordeaux to Paris, France.
